Title: Cryo-EM and directed evolution reveal how Arabidopsis nitrilase specificity is influenced by its quaternary structure

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Branch 1 nitrilase, nitrilase superfamily; helical oligomeric enzyme with conserved CEEK catalytic tetrad
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes β-cyano-L-alanine to the corresponding acid and/or amide with release of ammonia; plant NIT4 enzymes are highly specific for β-cyano-L-alanine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Wild-type AtNIT4 shows high specificity and activity toward β-cyano-L-alanine and little or no activity toward tested alternative nitriles
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cryo-EM structure determination; recombinant expression and purification; directed evolution comparison; nitrile activity assays; negative-stain EM
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtNIT4 is the main experimental gene and forms an active left-handed helical filament with helical twist about -73°. 2. Wild-type AtNIT4 is highly specific for β-cyano-L-alanine and inactive or nearly inactive on most alternative nitriles tested. 3. Structural analysis showed a lid loop over the active-site pocket, and residue R95 helps lock the loop via interaction with D317, linking quaternary structure to substrate specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro recombinant enzyme assays with nitrile substrates
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7/lac IPTG-inducible expression in pET-21b(+) in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Provides a scaffold for semirational engineering of plant nitrilases with altered substrate specificity for biocatalysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: About 50% of total activity of wild-type AtNIT4 may produce amide rather than acid according to prior biochemical evidence cited in the paper; atomic structure deposited as PDB 6i00 and EMD-0320

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Engineered branch 1 nitrilase variant, nitrilase superfamily
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes several nitriles not measurably converted by wild-type AtNIT4, producing ammonia in bacterial selection and enzyme assays
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: nitrile hydrolysis / engineered xenobiotic or alternative nitrile conversion
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: IPTG-induced heterologous expression in E. coli BL21
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Engineered triple mutant gains broad substrate specificity and loses activity toward the wild-type substrate β-cyano-L-alanine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-saturation mutagenesis; selection on minimal medium with nitriles; colony sequencing; purified enzyme activity assays; negative-stain EM reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Triple mutagenesis at R95, L169 and S224 yielded an AtNIT4 variant with broad substrate specificity. 2. The mutant hydrolyzed several nitriles not catalyzed by wild-type AtNIT4 and showed no activity toward β-cyano-L-alanine. 3. The mutant exhibited reduced helical twist from about -73° to -65°, supporting the link between quaternary structure and substrate specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Minimal media selection with 42 nitriles; in vitro enzyme assays
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7/lac IPTG-inducible expression in pET-21b(+) in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful template for semirational enzyme engineering and chemoenzymatic synthesis of nitrile-derived products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Helical twist changed to approximately -65° and filament diameter increased, resembling broader-specificity nitrilases

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Engineered branch 1 nitrilase variant, nitrilase superfamily
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Shows low activity toward 3-phenylpropanenitrile after disruption of lid-loop locking interaction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: IPTG-induced heterologous expression in E. coli BL21
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single R95T mutation opens the binding pocket sufficiently for low activity toward a larger nitrile but does not broaden specificity generally and does not change helical twist
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutagenesis; purified enzyme activity assay; negative-stain EM
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. R95T alone disrupted the proposed lock on the lid loop. 2. The single mutant retained approximately wild-type helical twist and lacked activity on β-cyano-L-alanine and most tested nitriles. 3. It showed low activity only for 3-phenylpropanenitrile, indicating lid opening alone is insufficient for broad specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7/lac IPTG-inducible expression in pET-21b(+) in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of native β-cyano-L-alanine activity without broad gain of function
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suggests R95 is a key engineering position but requires combination with additional mutations for broader specificity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Negative-stain reconstruction EMD-4804; helical twist remained about -73.3°
